heading: Mergers and consolidations of plans or transfers of plan assets
label: 1058 Mergers and consolidations of plans or transfers of plan assets
text: A pension plan may not merge or consolidate with, or transfer its assets or liabilities to, any other plan after , unless each participant in the plan would (if the plan then terminated) receive a benefit immediately after the merger, consolidation, or transfer which is equal to or greater than the benefit he would have been entitled to receive immediately before the merger, consolidation, or transfer (if the plan had then terminated). The preceding sentence shall not apply to any transaction to the extent that participants either before or after the transaction are covered under a multiemployer plan to which subchapter III of this chapter applies.September 2, 1974
notes: Amendments1980— substituted provisions respecting applicability of preceding sentence to transactions under a covered multiemployer plan to which subchapter III applies, for provisions relating to applicability of paragraph to a multiemployer plan only to extent determined by Corporation.Pub. L. 96–364
Effective Date of 1980 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except as specifically provided, see .Pub. L. 96–364section 1461(e) of this titleSept. 26, 1980
